value.Title: The U.S. is deploying giant spheres into the ocean and solving the challenges of harnessing one of the largest sources of sustainable energy

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGY-2F33-RVCY-N1H4-00000-00The U.S. is deploying giant spheres into the ocean and solving the challenges of harnessing one of the largest sources of sustainable energy2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:46ZLexisNexisThe U.S. is deploying giant spheres into the ocean and solving the challenges of harnessing one of the largest sources of sustainable energy


The United States has taken a decisive step toward harnessing wave energy by deploying an experimental system based on floating spherical structures in the ocean. This technology aims to convert ocean motion into a stable source of electricity and overcome the historical obstacles that have limited this renewable energy source.


The initiative is led by Panthalassa, a company specializing in ocean energy that has developed the Ocean-2 wave energy converter. The prototype has been tested in the waters of Puget Sound, where its structural strength, electricity generation capacity, and adaptation to the marine environment have been evaluated.
Wave energy is considered one of the most promising sustainable sources due to its consistency and power, but also one of the most complex to harness. Continuous waves, corrosion, biofouling, and maintenance costs have thwarted numerous projects in various countries.
Technology designed to adapt to the sea
Ocean-2 takes a different approach from traditional systems by moving with the natural motion of the waves rather than resisting it. Its modular architecture allows the components to flex and sway, reducing mechanical stress and improving durability against storms and irregular currents.
The device has a diameter of approximately 10 meters and is constructed from composite materials and rust-resistant alloys. Electricity is generated by converting the up-and-down motion of the waves using hydraulic systems and piezoelectric elements-a combination that minimizes the use of rigid, wear-prone parts.
Energy Output and Environmental Balance
During technical trials conducted in collaboration with Everett Ship Repair, the system reached peak output of up to 50 kilowatts under favorable conditions. According to satellite-monitored data, performance remained stable, even in scenarios with complex currents.
The low-impact design has also been evaluated from an ecological standpoint. The project's environmental lead, Dr. Liam Chen, stated: "Sustainability means living in harmony with the ocean," emphasizing that the tests have shown no significant disruption to marine life during the system's deployment.
The United States has taken a decisive step toward harnessing wave energy by deploying an experimental system based on floating spherical structures in the ocean. This technology aims to convert ocean motion into stable electricity and overcome the historical obstacles that have limited this renewable energy source.

value.Title: Microsoft may abandon one of its climate goals. Blame data centers. An hourly clean energy match may be out the door.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-98F3-S5K5-91R2-00000-00Microsoft may abandon one of its climate goals. Blame data centers.2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:47ZLexisNexisSCIENCEMicrosoft may abandon one of its climate goals. Blame data centers.An hourly clean energy match may be out the door.Neal Broverman

Microsoft stands out as one of the most environmentally responsible companies in Big Tech, but a new report indicates it may be retreating from one of its most ambitious climate goals.

The Redmond, Washington-based company previously pledged to match 100 percent of its hourly electricity use with renewable energy purchases by 2030; Microsoft already meets its annual targets for renewable energy matches.

Now, with pressure to construct data centers to fuel AI, Microsoft is considering delaying or abandoning that hourly goal, Bloomberg reports.

The hourly clean power goal is named 100/100/0, indicating the intent to match all of its energy use, all of the time, with zero-carbon energy purchases. That goal is complicated by the enormous amount Microsoft is spending on data centers, around $190 billion through the end of the year. The data center boom has equated to less money for other corporate endeavors, like clean energy. In the wake of those costs, Microsoft has already begun reducing its carbon-dioxide removal program.

Many tech companies are looking to natural gas which emits Earth-warming greenhouse gases to partially power their data centers, rather than cleaner energy sources like solar, wind, or hydro power.

The companies of Big Tech are all emitting more carbon in the wake of the AI race; Microsoft has seen a 23 percent jump in carbon emissions since late 2022, while Google's increased by 51 percent and Meta's by 64 percent during that same time period, Bloomberg reports.

value.Title: Weyerhaeuser plans new construction project in Gallatin , Tennessee
value.Overview: intelligence360 News, CONSTRUCTION NEWS & PACKAGING AND PAPER NEWS, 307words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-2BW3-SH2K-D1J3-00000-00Weyerhaeuser plans new construction project in Gallatin, Tennessee2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisCONSTRUCTION NEWS & PACKAGING AND PAPER NEWSWeyerhaeuser plans new construction project in Gallatin, TennesseeCraig Etkin

Gallatin, Tennessee - According to state and local development sources, Weyerhaeuser plans to invest $10 Million to build out 96,250 square feet of new space in Gallatin. The company is creating 20 new jobs at this facility. The company plans to occupy the new space at 615 Rappahannock Wire Road in Gallatin, on or about December 1, 2026. According to the company website For more than a century, weve been growing trees and making forest products that improve lives in fundamental ways. We manage our millions of acres of forests on a continuous and fully sustainable cycle. We are responsible stewards of our land for multiple uses, including recreation, conservation, economic development and different forms of renewable energy. And the wood products we make are used to build homes where families are sheltered and raised. Weyerhaeuser Company began more than 100 years ago with 900,000 acres of timberland, three employees and a small office in Tacoma, Washington. Founded in 1900 by Frederick Weyerhaeuser, weve grown to become one of the largest sustainable forest products companies in the world. In January 1900, Frederick Weyerhaeuser and 15 partners took a step that stirred the imagination and signaled a new era in the history of the lumber industry. In the largest private land transaction in American history to that time, they purchased 900,000 acres of Washington state timberland from the Northern Pacific Railway.

To learn more about Weyerhaeuser, visit http://www.weyerhaeuser.com/

value.Title: Reports from Pacific Northwest National Laboratory Describe Recent Advances in Environmental Risk (Development and Application of an Environmental Risk Register for Marine Energy Device and Project Developers)
value.Overview: Energy Daily News, ENVIRONMENT - ENVIRONMENTAL RISK, 606words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-WHF3-S7SW-M2WS-00000-00Reports from Pacific Northwest National Laboratory Describe Recent Advances in Environmental Risk (Development and Application of an Environmental Risk Register for Marine Energy Device and Project Developers)2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:46ZLexisNexisENVIRONMENT - ENVIRONMENTAL RISKReports from Pacific Northwest National Laboratory Describe Recent Advances in Environmental Risk (Development and Application of an Environmental Risk Register for Marine Energy Device and Project Developers)


2026 MAY 04 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Energy Daily News -- Data detailed on Environment - Environmental Risk have been presented. According to news reporting originating from Seattle, Washington, by NewsRx correspondents, research stated, "The marine energy industry is steadily advancing as more devices are deployed worldwide. However, several challenges and barriers remain, such as lingering uncertainty regarding the potential environmental effects of marine energy devices on marine animals, habitats, and ecosystems."


Financial support for this research came from United States Department of Energy (DOE).
Our news editors obtained a quote from the research from Pacific Northwest National Laboratory, "Concerns have led to difficulty navigating permitting and consenting processes and receiving authorization to deploy devices in the marine environment, including extended timelines and costs. Based on existing risk registers, a novel marine energy environmental risk register was created to help the marine energy industry move beyond these barriers. This risk register aims to aid marine energy device and project developers identify and assess potential environmental risks early in device design or project planning, document and track potential environmental interactions, prioritize risks and determine risk responses, and make decisions throughout device or project development. It can also be used as a tool to assist in communicating with regulators and advisors during permitting processes and to inform stakeholder and community engagement efforts. This paper details the methods and process to develop a risk register specific to environmental effects of marine energy and describes two use cases (one for wave energy and another for tidal energy) to highlight example results. Due to the tool's novelty, the paper showcases its application for the marine energy industry and acknowledges limitations and possible future improvements."
According to the news editors, the research concluded: "Overall, the environmental risk register shows promise to support marine energy developers when identifying, tracking, and addressing potential environmental risks and to help successfully navigate permitting and deploy marine energy devices responsibly."
This research has been peer-reviewed.
For more information on this research see: Development and Application of an Environmental Risk Register for Marine Energy Device and Project Developers. Ocean & Coastal Management, 2026;276. value.Title: Portland General Electric 10-Q: Capital Commitment to Hybrid Renewable Assets Driving Rate Base and 11 Other Management Decisions

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-8203-RTF0-P04G-00000-00Portland General Electric 10-Q: Capital Commitment to Hybrid Renewable Assets Driving Rate Base and 11 Other Management Decisions2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:37ZLexisNexisPortland General Electric 10-Q: Capital Commitment to Hybrid Renewable Assets Driving Rate Base and 11 Other Management Decisions

NYSE STOCK REPORT

Management finalized agreements under the 2023 All-Source RFP for two solar and battery hybrid projects totaling 615 MW. The company expects to invest roughly $1.03 billion in these assets, excluding AFUDC. The projects include full ownership of the 250 MW Biglow Optimization and partial ownership of the 365 MW Wheatridge Expansion. Both target commercial operation by the end of 2027. You benefit from concrete renewable capacity additions that secure expiring tax credits, advance decarbonization, and expand the rate base. These projects stem from a competitive procurement process designed to maximize customer value and system reliability.

Strategic Washington Expansion to Drive Revenue Diversification

On February 15, 2026, management agreed to buy specific Washington state utility operations from PacifiCorp for $1.9 billion. This move adds roughly 140,000 customers, 805 MW of generation, and 4,500 miles of lines across 2,700 square miles. The company will manage these assets as a separate subsidiary under Washington regulation. You benefit from increased scale and long-term growth without passing direct costs to existing Oregon or Washington customers. Management expects to close the deal in about twelve months, pending state approvals. While this introduces timing risk, committed financing and a joint-venture partner back the execution. This transaction aims to boost earnings per share and free cash flow while keeping majority ownership and operational control.

Joint Venture Formation to Optimize Balance Sheet Flexibility

Management formed a joint venture with Manulife Infrastructure Fund III, L.P. to hold a minority stake in the new Washington utility business. Manulife will provide up to $600 million in equity toward the $1.9 billion purchase. PGE retains majority ownership and sole operator status. This funding structure lowers our equity burden and maintains financial flexibility. It aligns with our strategy to fund the deal through a balanced mix of debt, equity, and partner capital. You gain a lower-risk expansion with less capital intensity, while the company keeps strategic control. The joint venture allows us to execute the acquisition without altering current customer rates.

Elevated Capital Expenditure Plan Advancing Grid Modernization

The 2026 capital expenditure plan reaches approximately $1.655 billion, excluding AFUDC. This spending targets grid modernization, renewable energy, and customer growth. Management expects capital deployment to stay elevated at $1.3 to $1.7 billion annually through 2030. You see direct value through rate-base growth that supports future earnings while meeting rising load demands and decarbonization targets. We plan to fund these investments using $1.0 to $1.2 billion in operating cash flows, alongside long-term debt and equity issuances. Management continues a disciplined capital deployment strategy that balances heavy investment needs with customer affordability.

Proactive Equity Financing to Support Capital Deployment

Management secured equity forward sale and at-the-market offering agreements to fund growth. A February 2026 agreement for 10.8 million shares could yield $530 million, building on $300 million raised through earlier programs. The company will use these proceeds for general corporate purposes, non-emitting capacity, and the Washington acquisition. While this causes modest dilution, you gain non-dilutive capital at settlement that protects credit metrics and limits excessive debt. Remaining forward positions give us the flexibility to time capital raises with actual spending. This approach highlights a prudent equity financing strategy during a cycle of elevated capital deployment.

Robust Debt Financing Strategy Preserving Liquidity

The company secured new debt funding, including a $350 million, 24-month term loan, drawing $170 million by mid-April 2026. Management also amended the acquisition bridge facility, reducing it to $1.3 billion. Along with revolving credit and commercial paper, our available liquidity stands strong at $954 million as of March 31, 2026. You benefit from this cost-effective, diversified funding that covers capital spending and the upcoming acquisition while protecting investment-grade credit ratings. Debt covenants remain secure, with debt-to-total capital at 53.1%. This strategic debt management keeps interest expenses manageable as we execute our capital plan.

Corporate Reorganization Initiated for Enhanced Financial Agility

In July 2025, management applied to the OPUC to create a holding company structure, placing a non-operating corporate entity above the existing utility. This shift provides financial agility for reliability planning, economic development, and the pending Washington acquisition. Shares will convert one-for-one, causing no change in relative ownership. You gain improved access to capital markets and a structurally efficient entity built for growth. The application is in the evidentiary phase, with an OPUC order expected in August 2026. This foundational corporate restructure holds significant long-term implications for our balance sheet and growth initiatives.

Resource Procurement Advancing Clean Energy Capacity Targets

Management sent the final 2025 All-Source RFP shortlist to the OPUC and began commercial negotiations. We are prioritizing renewable and non-emitting dispatchable capacity, targeting about 2,500 MW. The shortlist features battery, wind, solar, and hybrid projects using both power purchase and build-transfer agreements. This addresses 2029 capacity needs while advancing state decarbonization goals. You see accelerated progress toward clean energy through cost-effective resources that keep power affordable and reliable. We favor projects launching between 2028 and 2030, which can drive future earnings through owned assets. Management expects OPUC acknowledgment by May 2026.

Strategic Transmission Investment Expanding Regional Market Access

Management signed a non-binding memorandum of understanding to take a 20% stake in the North Plains Connector. This 415-mile high-voltage transmission project represents a $3.2 billion investment, offset by a $700 million federal grant. The line connects three regional markets, relieving congestion and addressing load growth. You gain from expanded transmission capacity that opens wholesale market access and boosts reliability. Federal funding helps lower the net cost of this infrastructure. We continue to negotiate with the Department of Energy, and final terms will dictate our material capital commitment. This investment strictly aligns with our integrated resource planning needs.

Long-Term Resource Strategy Reaffirming Decarbonization Targets

Management submitted an updated Clean Energy Plan and Integrated Resource Plan to the OPUC on June 18, 2025. The filing outlines a new Preferred Portfolio but leaves the previously acknowledged 2023 Action Plan intact. We refined our capacity needs to require 3,500 to 4,500 MW of renewable and non-emitting resources. You receive a clear view of our 20-year resource strategy, designed to hit 80% emission reductions by 2030 and 100% by 2040. This approach sustains our clean energy momentum without triggering immediate rate-case impacts. The plan underscores management's commitment to an equitable, affordable, and reliable energy transition.

Proactive Wildfire Mitigation Investments Protecting Asset Resiliency

The company filed its 2026-2028 Wildfire Mitigation Plan with the OPUC. Management forecasts $47 to $50 million in annual operating costs and $70 to $84 million in capital investments for system hardening and vegetation management. We already invested $14 million in related projects during the first quarter of 2026. You benefit from proactive risk mitigation that limits wildfire liability and reduces power shutoff events. We expect to recover these costs through rates, subject to prudency reviews. This plan proves management's dedication to public safety and financial defense in a high-risk environment.

Disciplined Regulatory Strategy Defending Cost Recovery and Margins

Management continues pushing to recover deferred costs through established regulatory mechanisms. We are actively contesting specific disallowances and earnings tests from recent OPUC orders. First-quarter results showed $15 million in charges from storm and cost disallowances. However, management believes we will recover the remaining $44 million in storm and $80 million in associated deferred balances. You should watch rate-case outcomes closely, as they directly impact earned returns and cash flows. We remain heavily engaged in regulatory dockets involving large loads and data centers to protect customer affordability and our bottom line. This disciplined approach smooths earnings volatility while defending essential capital investments.

value.Title: What are solar panels doing on the site of an old Pierce County landfill?

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ8-GWN3-RYVV-62GJ-00000-00What are solar panels doing on the site of an old Pierce County landfill?2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:47ZLexisNexisSTATE AND REGIONAL NEWSWhat are solar panels doing on the site of an old Pierce County landfill?Julia ParkThe Peninsula Gateway (Gig Harbor, Wash.)

If you’ve recently driven to the Purdy Transfer Station, you might have noticed something different.

In March, contractors finished installing a large array of solar panels on the southern part of the Purdy Landfill, near the waste and recycling center. The panels are expected to generate over 1 million kilowatt-hours of energy each year, which is “enough to power approximately 100 homes, or to drive an electric car around the Earth 120 times,” according to Pierce County’s website.

The solar array at 14515 54th Ave. NW will connect to the Peninsula Light Company grid and is funded through a $2.3 million grant from the state Department of Commerce Clean Energy Program. Pierce County spokesperson Michelle Kircher told The News Tribune April 27 that the county was working with Peninsula Light Co. to finalize the terms of their agreements.

“PenLight currently receives nearly all of our power through the Bonneville Power Administration, which provides clean, reliable energy that is largely carbon free,” PenLight spokesperson Britni Wickens wrote in a February email. “If interconnected, the County’s solar project would provide a small portion of our overall energy needs — estimated at about 0.2% of our total load.”

Wickens wrote that the solar project “would not impact members’ electricity rates.”

The county intends to invest their net revenue from the solar project into the community through “energy assistance and efficiency projects benefitting low-income residents on Key Peninsula,” the county’s website says.

It’s not clear yet what those will be. Pierce County staff are looking to gather input from residents.

“We will host workshops, distribute surveys, and conduct interviews to identify how the revenue should be used to ease energy burden, increase energy efficiency, and reduce pollution in Key Peninsula and beyond,” she wrote.

Why is Pierce County pursuing solar at this site? Kircher explained that the county is acting on feedback from the community.

In 2021, the Pierce County Council and then-County Executive Bruce Dammeier passed a plan to reduce countywide greenhouse gas emissions. “Transitioning to renewable energy, community solar projects, and increasing awareness of energy assistance programs are top community priorities,” the plan said. The county’s goal is to cut greenhouse gas emissions by 45% from 2015 to 2030.

The Purdy Landfill is 15 acres and operated from 1941 to 1989, according to a report from the Tacoma-Pierce County Health Department. For years, the landfill’s excavated pits accumulated sewage and household waste from residents of the Gig Harbor area and University Place. In the years following, crews covered the landfill with layers of soil and a synthetic liner and seeded the top with grass.

The 12-inch soil cap can’t be regraded or significantly disturbed, which is why the site works well for a ground-mounted solar array, the Pierce County website says, and Kircher said the spot gets good sun exposure.

Kircher wrote that the county is already using the site for education, hosting tours with Workforce Central and Goodwill as well as environmental science students from Clover Park Technical College.

Some solar arrays use what’s known as battery energy storage systems, or BESS, to store energy and provide back-up power to homes and businesses when needed. Some communities have raised concerns about the potential fire risk of these systems, though the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency reports that these incidents have been less common since 2020.

Kircher said that the Purdy solar project “will not utilize any kind of battery energy storage system (BESS) at this time.” The county is working on code to regulate BESS and where these systems can be installed.

“BESS elevate the usefulness of solar energy projects by capturing excess power when the sun is shining, then make that power available later when there may be higher demand,” she wrote. “The County could consider a BESS at this site in the future.”

Is the South Sound sunny enough for solar? Kircher said a common misconception is that Washington state isn’t well-suited for solar energy.

“The Pacific Northwest receives more sunlight annually than Germany - the leading global user of solar,” she wrote. “Our location north of the 45th parallel means we experience exceptionally long summer days compared to other parts of the country, and western Washington’s relatively cool climate (not getting too hot) is ideal for solar panels to operate efficiently.”

More information about solar energy is available on the county’s educational materials online.

value.Title: NW Pacific Electric Co Highlights Critical Need For Electrical Service Upgrades In Vancouver Area

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-3F73-SDH4-634G-00000-00NW Pacific Electric Co Highlights Critical Need For Electrical Service Upgrades In Vancouver Area2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:37ZLexisNexisNW Pacific Electric Co Highlights Critical Need For Electrical Service Upgrades In Vancouver Area

VANCOUVER, WA, May 08, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- VANCOUVER, WA - May 08, 2026 - - NW Pacific Electric Co, LLC today emphasized the growing importance of modernizing aging electrical infrastructure as homes and businesses throughout the Vancouver, WA region face increased power demands from electric vehicle charging stations, heat pumps, and other high-consumption devices.


The licensed electrical contractor in Vancouver, WA, notes that many properties built before 2000 operate with outdated panels and wiring systems that cannot safely accommodate today's electrical loads. These aging systems pose potential fire hazards and limit property owners from adopting energy-efficient technologies that require upgraded electrical capacity.

"Properties throughout the Vancouver area are experiencing unprecedented electrical demands that simply didn't exist when many of these buildings were constructed," said Jess, Lead Electrician at NW Pacific Electric Co. "We're seeing panels from the 1970s and 1980s still in service, struggling to handle modern appliances, let alone electric vehicle chargers or whole-home electrification systems. These outdated systems aren't just inefficient-they can become genuine safety concerns."
The push for electrical upgrades comes as more homeowners transition to electric heat pumps, install solar panels, and add electric vehicle charging stations to their properties. Commercial properties face similar challenges as they modernize their facilities with energy-efficient lighting systems, updated HVAC equipment, and enhanced data infrastructure requiring reliable power distribution.
Electrical panel upgrades have become particularly critical as insurance companies increasingly scrutinize older electrical systems. Some insurers now require panel replacements or refuse coverage for properties with certain outdated panel brands known for safety issues. Additionally, the National Electrical Code continues to evolve with stricter requirements for arc-fault circuit interrupters and ground-fault circuit interrupters that older panels cannot accommodate.
The company reports that electrical service upgrades can reduce energy consumption by 15 to 30 percent through improved efficiency and proper load distribution. Modern panels also provide better surge protection for sensitive electronics and smart home devices that have become standard in residential and commercial properties.
Beyond safety and efficiency benefits, upgraded electrical systems add significant value to properties. Real estate professionals increasingly recognize modern electrical infrastructure as a key selling point, particularly as buyers prioritize homes ready for electric vehicle adoption and renewable energy integration.
NW Pacific Electric Co, LLC serves the Greater Portland Vancouver metro area with comprehensive electrical services for residential and commercial clients. The company specializes in electrical panel services, lighting retrofits, commercial tenant improvements, HVAC wiring, and National Electrical Code compliance upgrades. Their team of licensed electricians provides emergency services, troubleshooting, and complete electrical system installations. Based in Vancouver, WA, the company has established itself as a trusted provider of professional electrical solutions, focusing on safety, efficiency, and code compliance for properties throughout Clark County and the surrounding region.
###
For more information about NW Pacific Electric Co, LLC, contact the company here:

value.Title: NW Pacific Electric Co Highlights Critical Need for Electrical Service Upgrades in Vancouver Area

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ0-WV63-RV8X-S346-00000-00NW Pacific Electric Co Highlights Critical Need for Electrical Service Upgrades in Vancouver Area2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:47ZLexisNexisNW Pacific Electric Co Highlights Critical Need for Electrical Service Upgrades in Vancouver Area

(GlobeNewswire) - VANCOUVER, WA - May 08, 2026 - -

NW Pacific Electric Co, LLC today emphasized the growing importance of modernizing aging electrical infrastructure as homes and businesses throughout the Vancouver, WA region face increased power demands from electric vehicle charging stations, heat pumps, and other high-consumption devices.

The licensed electrical contractor in Vancouver, WA, notes that many properties built before 2000 operate with outdated panels and wiring systems that cannot safely accommodate today's electrical loads. These aging systems pose potential fire hazards and limit property owners from adopting energy-efficient technologies that require upgraded electrical capacity.

"Properties throughout the Vancouver area are experiencing unprecedented electrical demands that simply didn't exist when many of these buildings were constructed," said Jess, Lead Electrician at NW Pacific Electric Co. "We're seeing panels from the 1970s and 1980s still in service, struggling to handle modern appliances, let alone electric vehicle chargers or whole-home electrification systems. These outdated systems aren't just inefficientthey can become genuine safety concerns."

The push for electrical upgrades comes as more homeowners transition to electric heat pumps, install solar panels, and add electric vehicle charging stations to their properties. Commercial properties face similar challenges as they modernize their facilities with energy-efficient lighting systems, updated HVAC equipment, and enhanced data infrastructure requiring reliable power distribution.

Electrical panel upgrades have become particularly critical as insurance companies increasingly scrutinize older electrical systems. Some insurers now require panel replacements or refuse coverage for properties with certain outdated panel brands known for safety issues. Additionally, the National Electrical Code continues to evolve with stricter requirements for arc-fault circuit interrupters and ground-fault circuit interrupters that older panels cannot accommodate.

The company reports that electrical service upgrades can reduce energy consumption by 15 to 30 percent through improved efficiency and proper load distribution. Modern panels also provide better surge protection for sensitive electronics and smart home devices that have become standard in residential and commercial properties.

Beyond safety and efficiency benefits, upgraded electrical systems add significant value to properties. Real estate professionals increasingly recognize modern electrical infrastructure as a key selling point, particularly as buyers prioritize homes ready for electric vehicle adoption and renewable energy integration.

NW Pacific Electric Co, LLC serves the Greater Portland Vancouver metro area with comprehensive electrical services for residential and commercial clients. The company specializes in electrical panel services, lighting retrofits, commercial tenant improvements, HVAC wiring, and National Electrical Code compliance upgrades. Their team of licensed electricians provides emergency services, troubleshooting, and complete electrical system installations. Based in Vancouver, WA, the company has established itself as a trusted provider of professional electrical solutions, focusing on safety, efficiency, and code compliance for properties throughout Clark County and the surrounding region.

###

For more information about NW Pacific Electric Co, LLC, contact the company here:

NW Pacific Electric Co, LLC

Jess

(360) 314-2544

Info@nwpacificelectric.com

NW Pacific Electric Co, LLC

Vancouver, WA 98664, United States

urn:contentItem:6JHV-R103-SD9Y-N1BK-00000-00Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) Reconsiders 2030 Renewable Energy Target as AI-Driven Data Center Buildout Intensifies2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:46ZLexisNexisMicrosoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) Reconsiders 2030 Renewable Energy Target as AI-Driven Data Center Buildout Intensifies

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

07 May 2026 15:15 EDT

Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) is evaluating whether to delay or abandon its plan to match hourly electricity use with renewable energy purchases by 2030, a discussion active as of 7 May 2026 amid a rapid expansion of AI-focused data centers. The deliberations reflect how the surge in demand from Copilot and Azure services could affect the practicality of the company’s climate commitments

SECTION 1 MICROSOFT RECENT PRESS RELEASES

SECTION 2 MICROSOFT ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 MICROSOFT RECENT PRESS RELEASES

December 04, 2025: Microsoft quarterly dividends steady

Microsoft today announced a quarterly dividend of 91.0c per share for Q2/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q1/2026) dividend of 91.0c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, February 19, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, February 19, 2026 and it is payable on Thursday, March 12, 2026.

December 04, 2025: Microsoft quarterly dividends steady

Microsoft today announced a quarterly dividend of 91.0c per share for Q2/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q1/2026) dividend of 91.0c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, February 19, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, February 19, 2026 and it is payable on Thursday, March 12, 2026.

September 17, 2025: Microsoft quarterly dividends up 9.6% sequentially from previous quarter

Microsoft today announced a quarterly dividend of 91.0c per share for Q1/2026; up 9.6% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 83.0c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, November 20, 2025 and the record date is Thursday, November 20, 2025 and it is payable on Thursday, December 11.

SECTION 2 MICROSOFT ACTIVITIES

Activities

Microsoft Corporation, founded in 1975 and headquartered in Redmond, Washington, is a global leader in technology, developing and licensing software, hardware, and cloud-based services. Its core business activities encompass operating systems like Windows, productivity tools such as Microsoft Office and Teams, enterprise solutions including Azure cloud computing, and hardware devices like Surface. The company also operates in gaming through Xbox and professional networking via LinkedIn. Among its fastest-growing segments are cloud services, particularly Azure, which has expanded rapidly due to rising demand for scalable computing, AI-driven solutions, and hybrid cloud offerings, contributing significantly to revenue growth. Additionally, subscriptions for productivity software and emerging AI integrations, such as in Copilot, are fueling innovation and market expansion, solidifying Microsoft's position in the digital transformation landscape. With a strong focus on security and sustainability, Microsoft continues to drive technological advancements for businesses and consumers worldwide. It is the NASDAQ's largest Computer software company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - UPDATES FROM PERKINS COIE AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-S443-S3V9-01PX-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - UPDATES FROM PERKINS COIE AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - UPDATES FROM PERKINS COIE AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS TAPS PILLSBURY TALENT TO DEEPEN BENEFITS PRACTICE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has added a prominent Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP attorney to its Employee Benefits team, reflecting a strategic response to rising complexity in benefits law.The attorney's background encompasses compliance and regulatory issues across employee benefits, with expectations to strengthen the firm's ability to provide comprehensive legal services.Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire supports Morgan Lewis' ongoing plan to expand its benefits practice in a changing regulatory environment, while Pillsbury has seen departures to other firms.The move underscores the competitive talent market in corporate law and signals Morgan Lewis' commitment to maintaining state-of-the-art capabilities for its diverse client base.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST AND PERKINS COIE LAUNCH GLOBAL FIRM ASHURST PERKINS COIE TO EXPAND CROSS-BORDER OFFERINGS

On 13th April 2026 the partnership vote to merge Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie was approved, with a closing target in the third quarter subject to customary conditions.The leadership structure will feature Bill Malley and Paul Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Karen Davies of Ashurst and Brian Eiting of Perkins Coie will serve as co-chairs.The merged group will comprise about 3,000 lawyers in 52 offices across 32 countries, with core hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York and a focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services.The combined revenues are around USD 2.8 billion, reflecting extensive cross-border experience across cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The aim is to sharpen competitive edge and position the firm for ongoing growth in the global market.

+ GLOBAL LAW FIRMS PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE UNDER ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union that combines geographic reach with sector depth and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will become a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion and about 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. The combined firm will be noted for its technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services capabilities, aligned with the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked that the partnership's approval signals a belief that the merger will deliver a differentiated global platform with scale and leadership in technology to meet cross-border client needs. 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform.'Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed the sentiment, emphasizing alignment between the firms and their joint future ambitions. 'This vote confirms our strong alignment and the compelling platform it creates for clients and talent alike,' he said. The parties noted that the merger will enhance cross-border service capability across key sectors.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET THROUGH SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has opened a Shenzhen representative office, signaling a renewed foothold in China. The office was inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026. The move follows the 2024 closure of the firm's Beijing and Shanghai offices and reflects a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to leverage the city's growing tech and manufacturing base in the Greater Bay Area. Bing Ai and Wei Yuan will lead the office as chief representative and managing partner and as representative and litigation partner, respectively; both are US-trained and bring deep experience advising technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management for semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, says the Shenzhen launch reinforces the firm's cross-border US legal capabilities and its aim to help clients navigate global markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY GRABS $60 MILLION SERIES B BACKING

Crosby has raised $60 million in a Series B, a mark of its rapid fundraising that was announced on April 3, 2026.The round was led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors, Bain Capital Ventures and angel investor Elad Gil, with Cooley LLP listed among the backers.Funds will be used to expand Crosby’s technology and support a growing client base that includes Clay, Rogo and Ramp.The company blends artificial intelligence with legal know-how to speed contract reviews and says the total value of its negotiated contracts now exceeds $1 billion, up from about $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year. In a blog post, Crosby wrote, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The firm has introduced simulations of counterparty responses to redlines, voice agents that negotiate for clients, and a dashboard for clients to oversee Crosby’s work. Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, with Daniels a former Cooley associate and Sarihan a former Google software engineer and Ramp tech lead.

+ COOLEY WELCOMES FRESHFIELDS VETERANS TO BOLSTER TECHNOLOGY GROUP

Cooley LLP announced a substantial extension of its technology practice through the addition of several attorneys formerly with Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. The move is designed to enhance Cooley's tech capabilities and underscores the firm's aim to remain competitive amid a dynamic legal environment.As of 23rd November 2025, the recruitment drive underscores the firm's continued push to expand technology offerings in a rapidly evolving sector. The infusion of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is expected to strengthen the existing practice and draw new clients needing technology-focused legal counsel.The incoming team members come with extensive experience advising leading clients on IP, data protection, and corporate tech transactions. Their expertise is expected to augment Cooley's well-established venture capital and startup advisory services, further cementing the firm’s leadership in technology law.Cooley's decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader market trend in the legal industry: firms actively pursue top talent to meet demands of a fast-moving, tech-centric market. As tech-related legal challenges grow more complex, the need for specialized knowledge becomes critical, making these hires timely.With this growth, Cooley aims to solidify its status as a preferred destination for technology clients, reinforcing its capabilities in an area essential for innovation and growth. As integration proceeds, observers will monitor the impact on Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH BOLSTERS PRIVATE EQUITY PRACTICE IN LOS ANGELES WITH NEW PARTNER

Reed Smith has announced the addition of a private equity partner in its Los Angeles office, strengthening its capability in a market that remains pivotal for private capital flows.The new partner joins on 4th May 2026 and brings deep experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services, aligning with the firm's plan to broaden its private equity practice and serve investment firms and corporate clients involved in M&A.The appointment underscores Reed Smith's aim to provide strategic insights and tailored solutions as the private equity landscape evolves, supporting sophisticated investment structures and regulatory considerations.This strategic hire positions Reed Smith to pursue growth opportunities in private equity and continue delivering high-quality service and durable client relationships.

+ REED SMITH ANNOUNCES PHILADELPHIA OFFICE LEADER FROM WITHIN

ReedSmith LLP has tapped an internal attorney to lead its Philadelphia office, signaling a strategic shift in a key market. The new leader has more than a decade with the firm and has contributed to notable cases and regional growth, with focus on client relationships and innovative solutions. Effective from 27th April 2026, the appointment reinforces Reed Smith's plan to bolster its local presence and maintain continuity across leadership, while expanding capabilities in the Philadelphia market.

+ REED SMITH ESTABLISHES FIRST BOSTON MANAGING PARTNER POSITION TO DEEPEN MARKET PRESENCE

ReedSmith LLP announced the appointment of its first Managing Partner in Boston, a move designed to intensify the firm's regional focus. The appointment is effective from 23rd April 2026. The Boston leadership will oversee local operations, with a mandate to deepen client relationships and drive growth across sectors in the Northeast. The firm said the decision reflects the region's rising demand for specialized legal services and its readiness to reinforce its presence with a dedicated leadership role. With extensive experience and a broad network, the new Managing Partner is expected to accelerate operational efficiency and strengthen Reed Smith's reputation in Boston, positioning the office as a key driver of the firm's long-term strategy.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ SPACE BIOTECHNOLOGY STARTUP BIOORBIT CLOSES $13.2M SEED ROUND; WSGR HELPS GUIDE FINANCING

BioOrbit has announced a seed round of $13.2 million, co-led by LocalGlobe and BREEGA, with contributions from Seedcamp, Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund, Type One Ventures, 7percent Ventures and others. The financing was announced on 30th April 2026.BioOrbit aims to establish a space-based pharmaceuticals factory within the next decade and is developing hardware to enable large-scale crystallization of protein drugs, a core step in biopharmaceutical manufacturing.The company is led by Cambridge scientist Dr. Katie King and oncology researcher Dr. Leonor Teles, who are spearheading this space-focused platform.Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. provided corporate governance and emerging-company counsel to BioOrbit during the financing, with the team including Daniel Glazer, Jose Campos, Rashad Mohammed and Sophia Kunthara.The deal underscores rising investor interest in space-enabled life sciences, and BioOrbit will share updates on LinkedIn and in UKTN coverage as plans progress.

+ WILSON SONSINI SUPPORTS OFF PISTE CAPITAL IN LANDMARK FUND DEBUT BACKED BY EDITION VENTURES

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. advised Off Piste Capital in forming its inaugural fund, Off Piste Fund I, which closed on 29th April 2026 with backing from strategic limited partners including Edition Ventures.The fund targets investments in companies building the systems and infrastructure of the future. Off Piste has already invested in four startups: Stendr, a Norway-based firm delivering AI-driven counter-drone technology; Objection, a UK startup creating accountability infrastructure for public information; Beyond Reach Labs, a US YC-backed venture developing deployable space structures; and Octet, a UK company specializing in cryptographic proofs of physical location.The deal team at Wilson Sonsini included Bradley Doline, Alice Cao, and Stephanie Yasay, whose work on capital markets and fund formation helped navigate the complexities of launching a VC fund. This collaboration underscores the firm's ongoing support for emerging venture activity and its expanding footprint in the tech investment landscape.

+ WILSON SONSINI ELEVATES ENERGY TRANSITION CAPABILITIES WITH KEN SANOCKI APPOINTMENT

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati has added Ken Sanocki as a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions practice, aimed at expanding the firm's ability to help clients finance energy infrastructure through the transition to cleaner power.Sanocki comes from Sunrun, where as vice president of legal he oversaw all project-finance matters, including tax equity financings and complex debt structures for distributed-energy projects. He led more than 50 tax equity financings totaling over $10 billion.Doug Clark, the firm's managing partner, said that Sanocki has operated at the center of the energy transition and will bring deep knowledge of tax equity and structured financings to clients navigating energy financing.In his new role, Sanocki will counsel on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure, assist with capital formation and strategic transactions, and help clients develop revenue-generating asset portfolios and prepare for IPOs or M&A. He began his career at Milbank after serving as SunEdison senior counsel for global structured and project finance; he earned a J.D. from NYU and a B.A. from Boston College.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN GUIDES TELIX IN 50:50 CO-DEVELOPMENT AND CO-COMMERCIALISATION DEAL WITH REGENERON WORTH USD 2.1 BILLION

Telix Pharmaceuticals has enlisted Sidley Austin to guide its collaboration with Regeneron, a deal valued at up to USD 2.1 billion that covers the co-development and co-commercialisation of next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies. The arrangement provides for a 50:50 split of costs and profits and includes an upfront USD 40 million payment for four initial programmes. Announced on 21st April 2026, the agreement is built to pool the companies' strengths in oncology and radiopharmaceuticals, aiming to move candidates through development more efficiently while sharing regulatory and governance responsibilities. Sidley partner Joshua Hofheimer noted that governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property are central to these collaborations, stating that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The alliance reflects a broader push in the biopharma space toward strategic collaborations designed to accelerate innovation in cancer care, a trend that law firms like Sidley are well-placed to support given the increasing complexity of cross-border deals.

+ RECRUITMENT PUSH DRIVES 33% GROWTH IN SIDLEY AUSTIN'S CITY OPERATIONS

Sidley Austin reported a 33% rise in City revenue, attributed to its hiring strategy as of 14th April 2026. Management credits a focused approach to identifying and integrating skilled professionals that expanded the City teams and diversified practice areas. The improvements in service have helped lift billable hours and client retention, reinforcing confidence in the hiring model. Analysts say the move demonstrates a willingness to invest in human capital to keep pace with a shifting legal market.

+ REGULATOR ALIGNS WITH TOP FIRMS AS CFTC CREATES INNOVATION TASK FORCE

The CFTC has formed an Innovation Task Force with Sidley Austin and Latham & Watkins to strengthen the regulatory framework for derivatives markets as technology reshapes the sector.The 11th April 2026 announcement notes the collaboration aims to incorporate insights from both firms to reflect evolving market realities, including digital assets and new trading technologies.Sidley Austin will provide regulatory guidance while Latham & Watkins contributes practical policy experience, helping the commission evaluate how tools like blockchain and AI intersect with oversight objectives.The task force is part of a wider effort to balance prudent supervision with fostering innovation, as the U.S. seeks to maintain leadership in global financial markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDEN ENTERTAINMENT BECOMES PRIVATE, FAMILY-OWNED IN TRANSACTION WITH GREENBERG TRAURIG

Greenberg Traurig advised Blake L. Sartini in moving Golden Entertainment to private family ownership, with the closing on 30th April 2026.Under the terms, Golden Entertainment shareholders received a cash dividend of $2.75 and 0.902 shares of VICI Properties Inc. for each share of Golden stock, providing liquidity while keeping an equity stake in the gaming and hospitality company.VICI Properties bought the real estate assets of seven Golden casino resorts, while a triple-net master lease was arranged with Sartini-controlled entities, enabling Golden to continue operating its full portfolio without disruption.Michael J. Bonner, Co-Managing Shareholder of Greenberg Traurig's Las Vegas office, remarked, "We were honored to represent Blake L. Sartini in returning Golden Entertainment to private family ownership. We congratulate Blake and the entire Golden Entertainment team!" The firm also highlighted its expertise across corporate transactions and real estate law, with a team including Dmitriy A. Tartakovskiy, Jim Mace, and James Doyle.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG IN TOKYO CHARTS AI-DRIVEN SHIFTS IN GLOBAL REAL ASSET MARKETS

Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office hosted a briefing for ANREV on Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities on the 30th of April, 2026. Panelist Joel H. Rothstein, who chairs the Asia Real Estate Practice, joined colleagues to discuss AI's effects on capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies, with emphasis on growing data centre activity in the Asia-Pacific amid digitalization. Rothstein stressed the firm's commitment to industry dialogue and noted that the conversations reflect the convergence of digital infrastructure, real estate, and emerging technologies. The team draws on Greenberg Traurig's global platform to support cross-border investments in real assets. Greenberg Traurig operates about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations and has been recognized for its real estate and infrastructure work, including a BTI Best of the Best honor in 2025 and listings on Am Law Global 100 and Law360 400.

+ LAW ROCKS LA: GREENBERG TRAURIG BAND THE MELTONES CLINCH TOP HONOR

On 29th April 2026, The Meltones, Greenberg Traurig, LLP’s house band, took top honors at Law Rocks Los Angeles, held at Whisky A Go Go in West Hollywood. The event raised more than $65,000 in donations and sponsorships, including $41,000 for The Headstrong Project.Over three nights, Law Rocks Los Angeles set a new fundraising benchmark with a total that surpassed $569,000, the highest in 15 years for the city. The Meltones opened with a Green Day cover, and victory was determined by audience support, ticket sales, and judges’ scores. GT was a Hall of Fame sponsor.The Meltones feature a cross-section of GT talent, with notable members such as Stephen L. Rabinowitz and Burt Bruton on guitar and Christopher A. Cernik on keyboard; vocalists include Michael R. Hogue, Ashley A. LeBlanc, and Tracy Newirth, with Tom Helf on drums and Daniel Blumberg on bass joining as guests. The band carries the legacy of co-founder Mel Greenberg and remains a core part of GT’s philanthropic ethos, which also includes a recent Law Rocks NYC win raising over $100,000 for The Door; the firm now employs about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESPERION'S $1.1B PRIVATISATION SIGNALS PRIVATE-EQUITY PULL IN BIOTECH, UNDER LEGAL HELM OF SIDLEY AND GOODWIN

Esperion Therapeutics Inc. has agreed to a $1.1 billion take-private, with Sidley Austin LLP and Goodwin Procter LLP leading the legal work. The announcement, made on 1st May 2026, positions the lipid-management specialist to focus on its programs outside the public markets.Private ownership is gaining traction in biotech as firms seek to streamline R&D and accelerate commercialization.Sidley and Goodwin's involvement reflects their strength in complex M&A deals and signals rigorous governance as Esperion transitions to a private structure.Analysts note that the move could enable longer-term strategic planning and potentially faster growth, while investors monitor the company's performance in its new privately held state.

+ FBT GIBBONS TAPS GOODWIN PROCTER VETERAN TO STRENGTHEN VC CAPABILITIES

FBT Gibbons is expanding its capabilities in venture capital by hiring from Goodwin Procter LLP for its New Jersey office. On 21st April 2026, the firm confirmed the recruitment of a venture capital attorney. The attorney comes with extensive venture capital experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, a leader in the field, and the firm plans to leverage that expertise for clients seeking guidance on capital raises. Industry observers note the hire reflects a broader trend of law firms sharpening niche practices to compete in a growing market.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER ENABLES CLOSURE OF $328 MILLION CONSUMER-FOCUSED FUND FOR TOPSPIN

Goodwin Procter LLP helped close Topspin Consumer Partners' latest fundraising, securing a $328 million commitment for a consumer-focused fund. The deal underscores a growing trend in private equity toward consumer-oriented strategies, with Goodwin Procter providing crucial legal support throughout the process.On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the fund’s closure, designed to pursue opportunities in the consumer sector. The firm’s counsel was instrumental in navigating fundraising requirements, maintaining regulatory compliance, and simplifying investor workflows.The fund intends to target a broad spectrum of opportunities across consumer industries, emphasizing segments with strong growth trajectories. With Goodwin Procter’s guidance, Topspin Consumer Partners aims to back innovative ventures well positioned to compete in a dynamic market.This partnership reinforces Goodwin Procter’s leadership in private equity work and its dedication to client success, with potential implications for investor confidence as the fund progresses.

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK WELCOMES SENIOR USPTO LAWYER AS IP PRACTICE GROWS

Orrick has expanded its IP practice by hiring a senior attorney from the USPTO, aiming to enhance capabilities in patent matters as the firm grows in tech and telecom.The new attorney will join on the 28th of April, 2026, and brings extensive patent examination and litigation experience.Orrick's leadership described the hire as evidence of the firm's commitment to top-tier IP services and to navigating the evolving IP rights landscape in the digital age.With demand for technology-focused counsel rising, the firm expects this addition to support clients facing complex patent challenges and strengthen its competitive standing.

+ ORRICK EXPANDS ONLINE SAFETY CAPABILITIES WITH NICOLE LOPEZ

Orrick has added Nicole Lopez, a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. Lopez previously held a significant position at Meta, and on Apr. 28, 2026, she expressed enthusiasm for the new role.Her hiring comes as 25 state legislatures weigh measures to safeguard minors online, a shift Kudon calls a watershed moment. "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large," Kudon said.Aravind Swaminathan praised Lopez as a multifaceted asset—"part policy expert, part litigation and regulatory strategist, and part business advisor"—and highlighted the Online Safety Law Center as a hub for guiding clients through rapid changes in the digital space.

+ FORMER META YOUTH SAFETY POLICY LEAD JOINS ORRICK

On Apr. 23, 2026, Orrick announced the addition of the former Meta youth safety policy head to its team, a move designed to expand its digital safety and compliance capabilities.The new hire comes with a strong track record in policy design and implementation, particularly around privacy and the protection of young users online.Orrick frames the appointment as part of its proactive response to evolving regulatory requirements governing youth online safety.The firm expects the hire to strengthen its advisory services as tech companies face heightened scrutiny and regulatory complexity.
