urn:contentItem:6JHV-R103-SD9Y-N1BJ-00000-00Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) Reevaluates 2030 Renewable Target as AI Data Center Demand Grows2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:46ZLexisNexisMicrosoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) Reevaluates 2030 Renewable Target as AI Data Center Demand Grows

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

07 May 2026 15:15 EDT

Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) is reassessing its 2030 goal to match 100% of its electricity consumption with renewable energy as AI data center power needs rise. On the 6th of May 2026, Microsoft Corporation outlined expansion plans that include significant investments in AI and cloud infrastructure, with multibillion-dollar projects underway in Europe, including Portugal.

Microsoft Corporation deepens AI partnerships, transforming its agreement with OpenAI into a nonexclusive structure. The move, while advancing AI technology, brings energy demand into sharper focus for its climate commitments and sustainability strategy. Investors will weigh potential effects on long-term operating costs and regulatory exposure as power needs intensify.

The reassessment could alter how investors evaluate AI growth against sustainability efforts and reputational considerations. Future updates are likely to discuss data center energy usage, European AI capital expenditures, analyst targets, and the current price-to-earnings ratio of 24.4 versus the Software industry average of 30.4. Insider selling has been significant over the past three months, introducing additional risk alongside potential energy and regulatory costs if climate targets are softened. Stakeholders will monitor how Microsoft Corporation navigates the intersection of AI expansion and sustainability.

SECTION 1 MICROSOFT RECENT PRESS RELEASES

December 04, 2025: Microsoft quarterly dividends steady

Microsoft today announced a quarterly dividend of 91.0c per share for Q2/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q1/2026) dividend of 91.0c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, February 19, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, February 19, 2026 and it is payable on Thursday, March 12, 2026.

December 04, 2025: Microsoft quarterly dividends steady

Microsoft today announced a quarterly dividend of 91.0c per share for Q2/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q1/2026) dividend of 91.0c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, February 19, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, February 19, 2026 and it is payable on Thursday, March 12, 2026.

September 17, 2025: Microsoft quarterly dividends up 9.6% sequentially from previous quarter

Microsoft today announced a quarterly dividend of 91.0c per share for Q1/2026; up 9.6% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 83.0c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, November 20, 2025 and the record date is Thursday, November 20, 2025 and it is payable on Thursday, December 11.

SECTION 2 MICROSOFT ACTIVITIES

Activities

Microsoft Corporation, founded in 1975 and headquartered in Redmond, Washington, is a global leader in technology, developing and licensing software, hardware, and cloud-based services. Its core business activities encompass operating systems like Windows, productivity tools such as Microsoft Office and Teams, enterprise solutions including Azure cloud computing, and hardware devices like Surface. The company also operates in gaming through Xbox and professional networking via LinkedIn. Among its fastest-growing segments are cloud services, particularly Azure, which has expanded rapidly due to rising demand for scalable computing, AI-driven solutions, and hybrid cloud offerings, contributing significantly to revenue growth. Additionally, subscriptions for productivity software and emerging AI integrations, such as in Copilot, are fueling innovation and market expansion, solidifying Microsoft's position in the digital transformation landscape. With a strong focus on security and sustainability, Microsoft continues to drive technological advancements for businesses and consumers worldwide. It is the NASDAQ's largest Computer software company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs. It is the NYSE's 24th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES

3.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

Press Release article 1 of 5, Source: Avista Corporation, 300 words

May 13: Regions first microgrid begins operations at Spokane community center

SPOKANE, Wash., May 12, 2026 -- Avista Utilities today announced the start of operations for the regionsfirst community-based microgrid. This marks the launch of an innovative energy system designed to help the Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Family Outreach Center (MLK Center) stay open and continue serving the community during extended, unplanned power outages.

The Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Family Outreach Center is a non-profit, community- based social service center located in East Central Spokane, one of the most ethnically diverse neighborhoods in Spokane County.

Located at the community center, the microgrid brings together solar power, battery storage and natural gas to provide added reliability when the power goes out, such as during extreme weather or other emergencies.

The systems solar and battery storage will also help the MLK Center reduce its standard energy bill, allowing for more resources to be redirected into critical community programs.

Were here for families every day, and during emergencies, people rely on us even more. This energy system helps us continue that work and keep our doors open for the East Central neighborhood, which means so much to me, said Freda Gandy, MLK Center executive Director.

On a typical day, the MLK Center will continue using power from the grid, supplementing energy from onsite solar and battery storage. During extended outages, batteries can help keep power flowing, with natural gas backup available if an outage lasts longer than a few hours. This helps ensure the Center remains open and operational.

The project was made possible through grant funding from Avistas Named Communities Investment Fund (NCIF) and grant support from the Washington State Department of Commerce.

Avistas NCIF program is the only one of its kind at a Washington utility.

May 13: Avista Regions first microgrid begins operations at Spokane community center

The system is designed to support the Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Family Outreach Centers year-round mission of providing food bank services, childcare services, and support for families

SPOKANE, Wash., May 12, 2026 -- Avista Utilities today announced the start of operations for the regionsfirst community-based microgrid. This marks the launch of an innovative energy system designed to help the Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Family Outreach Center (MLK Center) stay open and continue serving the community during extended, unplanned power outages.

The Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Family Outreach Center is a non-profit, community- based social service center located in East Central Spokane, one of the most ethnically diverse neighborhoods in Spokane County.

Located at the community center, the microgrid brings together solar power, battery storage and natural gas to provide added reliability when the power goes out, such as during extreme weather or other emergencies.

The systems solar and battery storage will also help the MLK Center reduce its standard energy bill, allowing for more resources to be redirected into critical community programs.

Were here for families every day, and during emergencies, people rely on us even more. This energy system helps us continue that work and keep our doors open for the East Central neighborhood, which means so much to me, said Freda Gandy, MLK Center executive Director.

On a typical day, the MLK Center will continue using power from the grid, supplementing energy from onsite solar and battery storage. During extended outages, batteries can help keep power flowing, with natural gas backup available if an outage lasts longer than a few hours. This helps ensure the Center remains open and operational.

The project was made possible through grant funding from Avistas Named Communities Investment Fund (NCIF) and grant support from the Washington State Department of Commerce.

Avistas NCIF program is the only one of its kind at a Washington utility.

May 06: Avista Corp. Board Declares Common Stock Dividend

SPOKANE, Wash., May 06, 2026 -- Avista Corp.s (NYSE: AVA) board of directors has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.4925 per share on the companys common stock, yielding an annualized dividend of $1.97. The common stock dividend is payable June 12, 2026, to shareholders of record at the close of business on May 19, 2026.

The declaration of dividends is at the sole discretion of the board of directors. The board considers the level of dividends on a regular basis, taking into account numerous factors, including financial results, business strategies, and economic and competitive conditions.

May 05: Avista Corp. Reports Q1 2026 Financial Results, Confirms 2026 Utility Earnings Guidance

SPOKANE, Wash., May 05, 2026 -- Avista Corp. (NYSE: AVA) today reported net income based on GAAP of $92 million, or $1.11 per diluted share, for the first quarter of 2026, compared to $79 million, or $0.98 per diluted share, in 2025. Non-GAAP utility earnings1 were $91 million, or $1.10 per diluted share, compared to $82 million, or $1.01 per diluted share in 2025. Avista Corp. is confirming its 2026 non-GAAP utility earnings guidance2 with a range of $2.52 to $2.72 per diluted share.

CEO Perspective

Strong performance in the first quarter demonstrates our focus on fundamentals: safety, reliability, and sound operational and financial execution. Our continued investments ensure we meet the needs of the communities we serve and also build long-term value for our customers, communities and shareholders. We are on track to meet our 2026 earnings guidance and are confident in the opportunities ahead, said Heather Rosentrater, President and CEO of Avista.

Analysis of First Quarter 2026 GAAP Earnings

Net income for the first quarter of 2026 increased compared to the first quarter of 2025 primarily due to increased utility margin resulting from the effects of our general rate cases and net investment gains at our other businesses compared to net investment losses in the first quarter of 2025.

Analysis of 2026 Non-GAAP Utility Earnings

The following table presents the changes in non-GAAP utility earnings and non-GAAP utility earnings per diluted share for the first quarter of 2026, as compared to the first quarter of 2025. It also outlines the various after-tax factors that contributed to these changes (dollars in millions, except per-share data):

(a)The tax impact of each line item was calculated using Avista Corp.'s federal statutory tax rate of 21 percent.

(b)Electric utility margin decreased as a result of the removal of revenues related to the recovery of Colstrip costs, partially offset by other effects of our general rates cases.

urn:contentItem:6JHV-2T23-S4NP-P04S-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:56ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Avista (NYSE:AVA), announced net profit of $92.0m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 30% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 16% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 27.6% sequentially from 87.0c in Q4/2025 to $1.11 in Q1/2026.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 16.5% from $79.0m to $92.0m

- EPS growth 13.3% from $0.98 to $1.11

- EBIT to Sales was up from 21.1% to 25.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 14.7% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 12.8% to 16.1%

Unfavourable Changes:

- Total revenue was down 7.6% from $617.0m to $570.0m

- Interest expenses to Sales was up from 6.3% to 7%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 1.9% to 2.3%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Dec 202530 Sep 2025EPS, c1118736.0Sequential growth in EPS %28142112Revenue, $ million570533403Sequential growth in Revenue %6.932.3-1.9Net Profit, $ million92.071.029.0Sequential growth in Net Profit %29.6145107

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 13.3%, Revenue was down 7.6% and Net Profit was up 16.5%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, c11198PCP growth in EPS %137.7Revenue, $ million570617PCP growth in Revenue %-7.61.3Net Profit, $ million92.079.0PCP growth in Net Profit %16.511.3

ACTIVITIES:

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs.

MARKET POSITION:

Avista Corporation (NYSE:AVA), the NYSE's 21st largest Utility company by market cap, increased $1.01 (or 2.5%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $40.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -25.1%. Average daily volume of 661,092 shares in the trailing year was 1.2 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-YV13-SHFW-V4WN-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:56ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Avista (NYSE:AVA), announced net profit of $92.0m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 30% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 16% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 27.6% sequentially from 87.0c in Q4/2025 to $1.11 in Q1/2026.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 16.5% from $79.0m to $92.0m

- EPS growth 13.3% from $0.98 to $1.11

- EBIT to Sales was up from 21.1% to 25.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 14.7% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 12.8% to 16.1%

Unfavourable Changes:

- Total revenue was down 7.6% from $617.0m to $570.0m

- Interest expenses to Sales was up from 6.3% to 7%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 1.9% to 2.3%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Dec 202530 Sep 2025EPS, c1118736.0Sequential growth in EPS %28142112Revenue, $ million570533403Sequential growth in Revenue %6.932.3-1.9Net Profit, $ million92.071.029.0Sequential growth in Net Profit %29.6145107

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 13.3%, Revenue was down 7.6% and Net Profit was up 16.5%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, c11198PCP growth in EPS %137.7Revenue, $ million570617PCP growth in Revenue %-7.61.3Net Profit, $ million92.079.0PCP growth in Net Profit %16.511.3

ACTIVITIES:

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs.

MARKET POSITION:

Avista Corporation (NYSE:AVA), the NYSE's 21st largest Utility company by market cap, increased $1.01 (or 2.5%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $40.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -25.1%. Average daily volume of 661,092 shares in the trailing year was 1.2 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JK2-RP43-RXTC-12JT-00000-00TrueBlue, Inc. (NYSE:TBI) Board Asked to Consider HireQuest’s All-Cash $105 Million Bid for On-Demand Assets2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:46ZLexisNexisTrueBlue, Inc. (NYSE:TBI) Board Asked to Consider HireQuest’s All-Cash $105 Million Bid for On-Demand Assets

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

14 May 2026 03:45 EDT

The all-cash proposal for this portion of People-Ready represents a separate path for TrueBlue. Hermanns noted that the on-demand business, which excludes the higher-growth skilled and solar/renewable energy staffing segments, has been an underperformer for TrueBlue for years. He added, "HireQuest's franchise model is uniquely suited to unlock the value in the on-demand portion of People-Ready. This deal could allow TrueBlue to divest of this underperforming segment and raise a substantial amount of cash that could be used to repay debt, grow and diversify its business strategically, or even make a sizeable special dividend to TrueBlue shareholders. Based on disclosures in their most recent proxy statement, this proposal represents $3.45 per share of TrueBlue stock."

May 3rd, 2026 (Alternet — Delivered by Newstex)

I'm not going to dwell on this week's outrages by Trump and his regime — his Supreme Court appointees gutting the Voting Rights Act, his bizarre claim that his war doesn't require congressional approval because it's #8216;terminated,#8217; his murders of additional sailors he suspects of smuggling drugs, his ongoing ICE raids, his continuing efforts to use the Justice Department to punish perceived enemies, his renewed attempts to silence comedians such as Jimmy Kimmel, his giant self-aggrandizing triumphal arch (what triumph?) and grotesque ballroom, his never-ending corruption and lies.

Yet in the midst of this cataclysm, the backlash against Trump is growing. Your work has been critical — your demonstrations and No Kings marches, your letters and phone calls to Congress, your boycotts and your local activism. Trump's approval ratings are in the cellar. Two-thirds oppose his war. Many are demanding an end to his reign. Even some MAGA faithful are turning on him.

But it's more than resisting Trump.

A new progressivism is being born.

Many of you are leading this. You are the activists in Montana and Hawaii pushing a new vision for reining in corporate power. You're the activists in California and New York devising new ways to tax the super-wealthy to pay for what most people need.

You're the activists in Massachusetts taking the lead on reproductive rights and access to health care. In Washington state, pushing new and more aggressive environmental regulations and renewable energy. In San Diego, Oakland, and San Francisco, devising new ways to address homelessness. Organizations like Voz in Portland, Oregon, empowering immigrant day laborers.

You are the activists in red states refusing to let warehouses be turned into migrant detention centers or let their towns host giant AI data centers.

You're raising the minimum wage across America. Since the start of this year, 22 states have raised it, bringing the total number of jurisdictions raising the minimum — including cities and counties — to almost 90, with 18 states and the District of Columbia now mandating $15 an hour or more.

You're supporting a new generation of young progressives in Congress: Maxwell Frost, Summer Lee, Greg Casar, Delia Ramirez, and the just-elected Analilia Mejia. And new progressive local leaders like New York's mayor Zohran Mamdani.

You're encouraging a new cohort of progressive rising stars, including Minneapolis's Omar Fateh, Tennessee's Aftyn Behn, Wisconsin's Francesca Hong, Illinois's Kat Abughazaleh, Texas's James Talarico, San Francisco's Saikat Chakrabarti, Maine's Graham Platner, Tennessee's Justin Pearson, Michigan's Abdul El-Sayed, Florida's Elijah Manley, and many others.

In my six decades in and around American politics, I've never seen anything close to the progressive talent and energy that's now emerging.

Make no mistake. These are terrible times — the worst I've lived through, and I've lived through some bad ones. (Remember 1968? Nixon's enemies list? Anyone old enough to recall Joe McCarthy's communist witch hunts?) Trump and his goons are doing everything they can to destroy America and much of the world.

But out of the embers and rubble that Trump and his despicable regime have wrought, a new America is being born. We are making it happen.

As long as we are alive and as long as we are resolved, as long as we are taking action to stop the worst of this catastrophe and also trying to make America and the world better, have no doubt: We will prevail.

Be safe. Hug your loved ones. Never, ever give up.

Robert Reich is a professor of public policy at Berkeley and former secretary of labor. His writings can be found at https://robertreich.substack.com
